subject: fail-closed queue freshness witness validator

observed: CM-0023 defined proposal, review, application, and post-application freshness witnesses but no executable validator existed.
operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json remains an older canonical projection while additive queue-update commits continue afterward.
inferred: A reusable validator reduces the risk that a later canonical queue reconciliation accepts stale, incomplete, or internally inconsistent witness data.
proposed: Use operations/continuity/queue-freshness-witness.mjs before any authorized canonical queue reconciliation.
superseded: Treating narrative agreement with CM-0023 as equivalent to machine-enforced witness validation.
unresolved: The repository still lacks a recursive queue-update tree enumerator and authorized reviewer policy.
This artifact does not authorize or perform a canonical queue mutation.

contract: proposal, review, and application head commits must match
proposal, review, and application queue-update tree digests must match
each traversal must declare complete enumeration and no traversal errors
timestamps must be parseable UTC ISO-8601 values
post-application verification must pass and retain canonical blob identity

verification.route: isolated Node.js runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

privacy_boundary: No private chat text, health or pet-health material, credentials, contact data, or unreviewed uploads were incorporated.
limits: The validator checks supplied witnesses but does not compute the queue-update tree digest.
Reviewer authorization is asserted as a non-empty identity string; no signature or allowlist policy is enforced.
No GitHub Actions execution is claimed.
